title: Repair Standalone DiT Pipeline CFG Alignment
summary: Repair a buggy standalone DiT sampling pipeline so its classifier-free guidance behavior matches the intended reference implementation while preserving the one-file edit workflow.
taskPrompt:
You are repairing a standalone Diffusers-based DiT pipeline on a Linux VM.

Task directory:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base`

Visible files:
- Buggy starter pipeline: `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/pipeline_dit.py`
- Raw sampling harness context: `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/sample.py`
- Raw run script context: `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/run_sample.sh`
- Benchmark-owned prompt: `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/task_prompt.md`

Your task:
1. Read `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/task_prompt.md` first.
2. Repair the standalone starter file at `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/input/pipeline_dit.py`.
3. Keep the fix localized to the pipeline file.
4. Save exactly one final file to:
   `/media/user/data/agenthle/computing_math/dit_pipeline_cfg_alignment_fid_256_001/base/output/pipeline_dit.py`

Do not modify files under `input/`.
Do not rely on hidden evaluator files while solving.
agentMustDo: Read the benchmark-owned task prompt and inspect the buggy standalone `pipeline_dit.py` plus the raw context files `sample.py` and `run_sample.sh`.
Repair the standalone DiT pipeline so the guidance behavior matches the intended reference behavior.
Keep the fix localized to the pipeline file rather than replacing the broader workflow.
Write exactly one final file to `output/pipeline_dit.py`.

inputFiles.name: sample.py
inputFiles.description: Raw sampling harness context from the submission.

inputFiles.name: run_sample.sh
inputFiles.description: Raw launch script context from the submission.

evaluation: The evaluator is binary. It reads the submitted `output/pipeline_dit.py` plus the hidden `reference/pipeline_dit.py`, then compares their behavior on deterministic CPU-only hidden tests using benchmark-owned diffusers stubs and real torch tensors. Hard failures include a missing output file, failure to import as a standalone top-level module, missing `DiTPipeline`, or any hidden behavior mismatch. Positive fixture score: `1.0`. Negative fixture score: `0.0`.
